name: Vantor (fleet)

overview.value: Vantor operates a constellation of 10 imaging satellites, described by the company as able to revisit the same location on Earth 15 times per day, with downlink speeds as fast as 15 minutes after collection. The fleet can collect the equivalent of Australia's landmass every day, including more than 3.5 million square kilometers at 30cm resolution, which Vantor says is the most capacity of any commercial provider. The constellation includes WorldView Legion, WorldView-3, WorldView-2, GeoEye-1 and WorldView-1, and Vantor also integrates partner systems such as Satellogic and Umbra into what it calls a virtual constellation.

notes: Fleet parent for Vantor (formerly Maxar Intelligence): WorldView legacy and WorldView Legion. Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05); fields await sourced fill by the weekly maintenance run.

events.headline: MDA acquires DigitalGlobe and rebrands as Maxar Technologies (per Via Satellite)
WorldView-4 fails after control moment gyro breakdown ends imaging capability (per Via Satellite)
Advent International and BCI complete acquisition of Maxar Technologies
Maxar launches first two WorldView Legion satellites after years of delay (per Spaceflight Now)
Maxar Intelligence rebrands as Vantor and unveils Tensorglobe platform
